inputs.label: Direct Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage including benefits and payroll taxes. Based on industry standards (Bureau of Labor Statistics).

inputs.label: Overhead Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of direct labor cost allocated to overhead (facilities, utilities, indirect labor). Typical range 100-200% in manufacturing.

inputs.label: Machine Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Depreciation, maintenance, and energy cost per machine hour. Based on equipment lifecycle costing.

inputs.label: Material Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct material cost per unit produced. Includes raw materials and purchased components.

inputs.label: Production Rate per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Number of units produced per hour. Based on standard time studies (Maynard).

inputs.label: Defect Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of defective units. Six Sigma target < 0.34% (3.4 DPMO).

inputs.label: Utilization Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of available time that equipment or labor is productive. World-class manufacturing target > 85% (OEE).

inputs.label: Profit Margin
inputs.businessContext: Desired profit margin on shop rate. Industry average 15-25%.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data. Affects risk adjustment in final rate.

thresholds.defectRate > 5: High defect rate; consider process improvement (Six Sigma).
thresholds.overheadRate > 200: Overhead rate exceeds typical range; review indirect costs.

hiddenLossDrivers: defectRate > 5 ? 'High defect rate increases cost per good unit' : ''
suggestedActions: defectRate > 5 ? 'Implement SPC and root cause analysis to reduce defects' : ''
utilizationRate < 70 ? 'Conduct OEE analysis to improve equipment effectiveness' : ''
overheadRate > 200 ? 'Review overhead allocation and reduce non-value-added activities' : ''
